engineGuide.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo XC60 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse D4 code for P3 XC60; high UK volume but injector seal leaks drive most replacement enquiries.
compatibleVariants: D4 Momentum, D4 Inscription, D4 R-Design, D4 AWD Geartronic, D4 FWD Manual
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks allowing diesel into sump (70k-110k miles)
EGR cooler clogging causing reduced power and DPF issues
Timing belt tensioner wear; catastrophic if belt snaps
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T5 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D4 code for late P3/P5 XC60; improved seals but timing belt discipline remains critical.
compatibleVariants: D4 Momentum Pro, D4 Inscription Pro, D4 R-Design Pro, D4 AWD Geartronic
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks (less frequent than D4204T5 but still occurs)
AdBlue system faults triggering limp mode
Timing belt replacement due at 10 years/100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T14 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: The iconic five-cylinder D5; smooth torque but piston ring wear defines its replacement story.
compatibleVariants: D5 SE, D5 Sport, D5 R-Design AWD, D5 Inscription Geartronic, D5 Executive
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing excessive oil consumption (90k-140k miles)
Timing belt failure if interval missed; interference engine
Turbo actuator sticking causing boost issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T10 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Lower-output D4 variant for entry-level XC60 trims; shares core architecture with D4204T5.
compatibleVariants: D3 Momentum, D3 Inscription, D3 SE, D3 AWD Geartronic
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks (same root cause as D4204T5)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey vehicles
DPF regeneration issues if used predominantly for town driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T11 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The mainstream T5 Drive-E code; strong sales but carbon buildup on intake valves drives maintenance needs.
compatibleVariants: T5 Momentum, T5 Inscription, T5 R-Design, T5 AWD Geartronic
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring walnut blasting (60k+ miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle on cold start

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear triggering lean codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T19 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Twin-charged T6 code delivering 310hp; complex supercharger+turbo system creates specific failure points.
compatibleVariants: T6 Momentum, T6 Inscription, T6 R-Design AWD, T6 Polestar Engineered
commonFailures: Supercharger electromagnetic clutch failure (60k-95k miles)
Turbocharger oil seal leaks causing smoke on overrun
Intercooler condensation issues in cold climates
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T29 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early T5 Drive-E code for first P5 XC60s; shares architecture with later B4204T19 but earlier production tolerances.
compatibleVariants: T5 Momentum (early P5), T5 Inscription (early P5), T5 R-Design (early P5)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing startup rattle
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
PCV system failure causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T11 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: B6 mild-hybrid code with 48V system; newest petrol XC60 engine entering replacement window.
compatibleVariants: B6 Core, B6 Plus, B6 Ultimate, B6 AWD Mild Hybrid
commonFailures: 48V belt-starter generator failure triggering hybrid faults
Carbon buildup on intake valves (shared with other Drive-E petrol)

entries.title: voltage DC-DC converter issues in mild-hybrid circuit
entries.cta: Get quotes for B420T Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Plug-in Hybrid
entries.familyHeading: T8 Twin Engine Plug-in Hybrid Family (2017-2025)
entries.history: The definitive T8 code combining Drive-E petrol with ERAD electric rear axle; ERAD failures dominate replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: T8 Twin Engine Inscription, T8 Recharge R-Design, T8 Polestar Engineered, T8 Extended Range
commonFailures: ERAD rear electric motor bearing failure (40k-80k miles)

entries.title: voltage inverter faults triggering hybrid system warnings
entries.familyHeading: T8 Twin Engine Plug-in Hybrid Family (2017-2025)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T48 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Performance Plug-in Hybrid
entries.familyHeading: T8 Twin Engine Plug-in Hybrid Family (2017-2025)
entries.history: Polestar Engineered variant of T8 with performance software and hardware upgrades; rare, high-value replacement cases.
compatibleVariants: T8 Polestar Engineered (limited production), T8 Recharge Polestar
commonFailures: ERAD rear motor failure (same as standard T8 but higher stress)
Performance software calibration issues after battery replacement
Upgraded brake system wear due to higher performance use
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T26 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated six-cylinder for early XC60; reliable but thirsty, now mostly high-mileage replacement cases.
commonFailures: Oil sludge if extended service intervals used
Variable valve timing solenoid failure causing rough idle
Plastic coolant component cracks leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6324S Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged six-cylinder T6 for pre-Drive-E XC60; strong performance but turbo seal failures drive replacements.
compatibleVariants: T6 R-Design AWD, T6 SE Lux Geartronic, T6 Executive AWD
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal leaks causing blue smoke on overrun

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear triggering lean codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6304T4 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo XC60's 2.0L Drive-E diesel/petrol and legacy 2.4L D5 engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare realistic repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Injector Seal Failure on D4204T5/D4204T14 - Why XC60 D4 Engines Leak Diesel
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC60 D4 Momentum/Inscription/R-Design (2013-2021, D4204T5 & D4204T14)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector seals degrade under heat cycling, allowing diesel to leak into the sump or onto hot engine surfaces - a known weakness in early Drive-E D4 units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace seals and copper washers, recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before fuel dilution damages bearings; 20k-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injector(s) with OEM or quality aftermarket units, full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; suitable if block/pistons remain undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D4204T5/T14 with upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil dilution has occurred or mileage exceeds 120k; prevents cascading failure

problems.recommendation: On a Volvo XC60 D4 with confirmed injector leak but no oil dilution, seal replacement offers good value. If the dipstick smells of diesel or the engine has exceeded 100k miles, replacement with an upgraded rebuilt unit is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T5 engine prices for your Volvo XC60

problems.h4: Piston Ring Wear on D5244T10 - Why XC60 D5 Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Factory piston rings wear prematurely under high cylinder pressures, allowing oil to pass into the combustion chamber - a well-documented issue on the 2.4L five-cylinder D5.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals, clean carbon, adjust valve clearances
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation only; does not address ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace rings and bearings, machine head; labour-intensive
repairOptions.longevity: Viable on low-mileage examples with otherwise healthy block; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D5244T10 with upgraded rings, balanced assembly, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective solution for high-mileage D5s; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: Piston ring wear on the D5 is progressive and rarely reversible. Unless the XC60 has very low mileage and exceptional service history, a rebuilt replacement engine with upgraded components offers better value and reliability than an in-situ ring job.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt D5244T10 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Catastrophe on D5/D4 - Why XC60 Engines Fail Without Warning
problems.rootCause: Interference-design diesel engines where a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike valves, resulting in catastrophic internal damage - preventable only by strict adherence to replacement intervals.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers and water pump per schedule
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done before 100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after belt failure damage; includes valve train rebuild
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once interference damage has occurred; includes upgraded components

problems.recommendation: There is no "repair" tier for a snapped timing belt on these interference engines - prevention is the only cost-effective strategy. If failure has occurred, replacement with a rebuilt unit is the only viable path to restore the vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 D4/D5 rebuilt engine prices before catastrophic failure

problems.group: Supercharger Clutch Failure on T6 B4204T29
problems.h4: Supercharger Clutch Failure on T6 B4204T29 - Why XC60 Twin-Charged Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: The electromagnetic supercharger clutch wears or fails electronically, disabling forced induction and triggering limp mode - a known weakness in the complex twin-charged Drive-E T6 system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, clutch coil replacement, wiring repair
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if failure is electrical; mechanical clutch wear requires full unit replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace supercharger assembly, recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses mechanical failure; suitable if turbo and engine internals remain healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B4204T29 with upgraded supercharger components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has suffered boost-related detonation or high-mileage wear

problems.recommendation: Supercharger clutch failure on the T6 is often a precursor to broader engine stress. If the XC60 has exceeded 80k miles or shows signs of detonation, investing in a rebuilt replacement engine with upgraded forced-induction components is the more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt B4204T29 Volvo XC60 T6 engine replacement

problems.group: ERAD Motor Failure on T8 B4204T48
problems.h4: ERAD Motor Failure on T8 B4204T48 - Why XC60 Twin Engine Hybrids Lose eAWD
problems.rootCause: The Electric Rear Axle Drive (ERAD) motor suffers bearing wear or inverter failure, disabling rear-wheel electric propulsion and triggering hybrid system faults - a high-cost failure unique to the T8 PHEV architecture.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Hybrid system scan, software update, inverter reset
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for transient electrical faults; does not address mechanical wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rear electric motor assembly, recalibrate hybrid control module
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses mechanical failure; requires hybrid-certified technician

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B4204T48 hybrid unit with upgraded ERAD components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage T8s or repeat failures; ensures full hybrid functionality

problems.recommendation: ERAD failures on the T8 are complex and expensive. Unless under warranty, a rebuilt hybrid powertrain from a specialist with T8 experience offers better value and reliability than piecemeal repairs. Always confirm hybrid certification before proceeding.
problems.cta: Enquire about rebuilt B4204T48 Volvo XC60 T8 hybrid engine availability

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo XC60
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your XC60 suffers injector leaks, oil consumption, timing belt failure, supercharger issues or ERAD faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo XC60 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo XC60 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo XC60 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volvo XC60 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volvo XC60 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L four-cylinder diesel dominates XC60 replacement demand, powering D3 and D4 variants across P3 and P5 generations. These Drive-E units offer strong economy but require strict maintenance to avoid injector and timing component failures.
commonFailurePoints: timing belt tensioner wear at 100k+ miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC60 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary five-cylinder 2.4L D5 powered early XC60 models with smooth torque and strong towing capability. Now entering high-mileage territory, piston ring wear and timing belt failures drive most replacement enquiries for this displacement.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC60 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Volvo's modular 2.0L petrol family powers T5, T6 and B-series XC60 variants. Turbocharged and twin-charged versions deliver strong performance but require careful maintenance to avoid carbon buildup and supercharger clutch issues on T6 units.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC60 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L turbocharged six-cylinder powered flagship T6 variants before the Drive-E transition. Rare but sought-after for smooth power delivery; replacement demand stems from turbo seal failures and high-mileage wear on early P3 examples.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC60 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 3.2L SI6 six-cylinder offered refined, linear power for early XC60 models. Reliable but thirsty; replacement enquiries typically relate to high-mileage wear or collision damage rather than inherent design flaws.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge if service intervals extended
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 3.2L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Volvo XC60 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The T8 Twin Engine combines a 2.0L Drive-E petrol engine with electric motors for plug-in hybrid capability. Complex architecture demands specialist handling; ERAD rear motor failures and high-voltage battery issues drive most hybrid replacement enquiries. ═══════════════════════════════════════
commonFailurePoints: ERAD rear electric motor bearing failure
coolant pump failure in hybrid circuit
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo XC60 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Volvo XC60 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powers most UK XC60s on the road; D4 2.0L and legacy D5 2.4L units dominate replacement enquiries due to high mileage and known failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel powers most UK XC60s on the road; D4 2.0L and legacy D5 2.4L units dominate replacement enquiries due to high mileage and known failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC60 Diesel Engine Families
families: D4204T5 - Most common D4 code (2013-2017), injector seal leaks at 80k+ miles
D5244T10 - Legacy five-cylinder D5, piston ring wear and oil consumption issues
D4204T14 - Late D4 (2017-2021), improved seals but still timing belt critical
D4204T11 - Lower-output D4 variant, similar failure profile to D4204T5
foundIn: P5 Generation (2017-2025) - D4204T14, D4204T23, D420T8 (B4 mild-hybrid)
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
D4 injector seal leaks requiring early attention
D5 piston ring wear leading to oil consumption above 100k miles
Timing belt replacement critical at 10-year/100k-mile intervals

items.descriptor: Petrol XC60s grew in popularity post-2015 with Drive-E T5/T6 units; replacement demand now rising as first-generation Drive-E engines exit warranty.

items.descriptor: T8 Twin Engine combines 2.0L petrol with electric motors for 390-455hp; specialist replacement required due to complex hybrid architecture and ERAD rear motor.

engineYears.h2: Volvo XC60 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volvo XC60's production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first XC60 launched with robust six-cylinder petrol and five-cylinder diesel options, establishing Volvo's premium SUV credentials.
keyChanges: XC60 model launched on P3 platform (shared with S80/V70)
Initial engines: 3.2L SI6 petrol, 3.0L T6 turbo petrol, 2.4L D5 diesel
AWD standard on most variants; Geartronic auto predominant
knownFor: Smooth, refined six-cylinder petrol character
D5 five-cylinder torque ideal for towing
Early examples now high-mileage; timing belt critical on diesel
Robust build quality but fuel economy lags newer Drive-E units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC60 D5 engine replacement • Volvo XC60 3.2 petrol engine failure • Volvo XC60 T6 turbo seal leak
enquiries: Volvo XC60 T6 turbo seal leak

years.description: Facelifted P3 XC60 introduced the efficient 2.0L Drive-E diesel D4, shifting replacement demand toward four-cylinder units.
keyChanges: Introduction of 2.0L Drive-E D4 diesel (D4204T5) alongside legacy D5
Updated infotainment and safety tech; minor exterior refresh
D4 became volume seller due to ULEZ compliance and economy
knownFor: D4204T5 injector seal leaks emerge as common failure point
D5244T10 piston ring wear drives oil consumption enquiries
Timing belt discipline critical on all diesel variants
Strong used market demand keeps replacement value high
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2017 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC60 D4 injector leak • Volvo XC60 D5 oil consumption • Volvo XC60 timing belt replacement cost
enquiries: Volvo XC60 timing belt replacement cost

years.description: All-new P5 XC60 on SPA platform introduced full Drive-E engine range, including T5/T6 petrol and first T8 plug-in hybrid.
keyChanges: New SPA architecture with improved safety and refinement
Full Drive-E engine lineup: T5 (B4204T19), T6 (B4204T29), T8 PHEV (B4204T48)
Diesel options reduced to D4 variants; D5 discontinued in UK
knownFor: Carbon buildup on Drive-E petrol intake valves becomes maintenance item
T6 supercharger clutch failures emerge at 60k-90k miles
T8 ERAD rear motor faults drive early hybrid replacement enquiries
Higher complexity demands specialist knowledge for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC60 T6 supercharger failure • Volvo XC60 T8 ERAD replacement • Volvo XC60 T5 carbon buildup

years.description: Volvo electrified the XC60 range with 48V mild-hybrid B4/B5/B6 codes, phasing out pure petrol/diesel options.
keyChanges: Introduction of 48V mild-hybrid system across B4, B5, B6 variants
T8 PHEV updated with larger battery and extended electric range
Diesel D4 discontinued in UK; B4 diesel mild-hybrid introduced
knownFor: 48V belt-starter generator failures emerge on early B-series units
Hybrid system complexity requires certified technicians for engine work
T8 Recharge ERAD failures continue but with improved software management
Replacement pricing reflects increased electrical system complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2022 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC60 B5 48V system fault • Volvo XC60 B6 mild hybrid engine replacement • Volvo XC60 T8 Recharge ERAD failure
enquiries: Volvo XC60 B6 mild hybrid engine replacement
Volvo XC60 T8 Recharge ERAD failure

years.description: Late P5 XC60 receives minor updates; replacement demand now focused on early Drive-E and first-gen hybrid units exiting warranty.
keyChanges: Minor software and equipment updates; no major mechanical changes
T8 Polestar Engineered variants continue with performance focus
Increasing number of 2017-2019 Drive-E units reaching replacement mileage
knownFor: First wave of P5 Drive-E engines now entering high-mileage replacement window
Hybrid battery health becoming key factor in T8 replacement decisions
Specialist hybrid-trained fitters in high demand for complex powertrains
Rebuilt engine market adapting to electrified component integration

faq.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volvo XC60 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Volvo XC60 engine typically costs £1,450-£8,900 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. D4 diesel units start around £1,450, while T8 hybrid powertrains reach £8,900 due to complexity.
keyPoints: D4 diesel (D4204T5) rebuilt: £1,450-£2,800 supply only
T8 hybrid (B4204T48) rebuilt: £4,200-£8,900 supply only
Supply & fit adds £650-£1,450 depending on workshop
All EngineMarket quotes include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 engine replacement prices for your exact variant

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Volvo XC60?
items.answer: Yes, if your XC60 has strong service history, low bodywork damage and under 150k miles. A rebuilt engine with warranty often costs less than a part-exchange loss on a newer replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: Rebuilt D4 engine costs ~£2k vs £8k+ part-exchange loss
Warranty-backed replacement preserves vehicle value
Ideal if chassis, interior and electronics remain in good condition
Not recommended if multiple major systems also need attention
items.cta: Get a tailored Volvo XC60 engine replacement quote to assess value

items.question: What is the most reliable Volvo XC60 engine?
items.answer: The 3.2L SI6 petrol (B6324S) and late D4 diesel (D4204T14) have the strongest reliability records. Avoid early D4204T5 without injector seal updates and T6 B4204T29 without supercharger clutch revisions.
keyPoints: B6324S 3.2L petrol: robust if serviced, but thirsty
D4204T14 late D4: improved seals over earlier D4204T5
B4204T19 T5: reliable with regular carbon cleaning
B4204T48 T8: complex; reliability depends on hybrid system care
items.cta: Compare prices for the most reliable Volvo XC60 engine codes

items.question: What causes Volvo XC60 D5 engine oil consumption?
items.answer: Excessive oil use in D5244T10 engines stems from premature piston ring wear, allowing oil to pass into the combustion chamber. This typically manifests between 90k-140k miles.
keyPoints: Piston ring wear is the root cause, not valve seals
Oil consumption worsens progressively; not a sudden failure
Rebuilt engines with upgraded rings resolve the issue permanently
Regular oil level checks can delay but not prevent the failure
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt D5244T10 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

items.question: Can I fit a used engine in my Volvo XC60?
items.answer: Yes, used Volvo XC60 engines are available from £950, but verify engine code match, compression test results and warranty terms before purchase to avoid costly compatibility issues.
keyPoints: Always confirm exact engine code matches your vehicle
Request compression test and service history documentation
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month parts-only warranty
Budget for ancillary renewal (mounts, hoses, belts) during fitment
items.cta: Check used Volvo XC60 engine availability with verified mileage

items.question: Does a replacement XC60 engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Volvo XC60 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on all rebuilt units
Used engines typically have shorter, parts-only coverage
Warranty covers internal components; ancillaries excluded
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 engine warranty terms across UK suppliers

items.question: How long does a Volvo XC60 engine replacement take?
items.answer: A standard Volvo XC60 engine swap takes 3-5 working days for supply & fit, plus 1-2 days for ECU coding and road testing. Hybrid T8 units may require additional time for high-voltage system calibration.
keyPoints: Diesel/petrol D4/T5: 3-4 days typical turnaround
T6 twin-charged/T8 hybrid: 4-6 days due to complexity
Mobile fitters may offer faster turnaround for D4 units
Allow extra time for ECU adaptation and emissions testing
items.cta: Find Volvo XC60 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Are XC60 D4 engines reliable?
items.answer: D4 engines are generally reliable with strict maintenance, but injector seal leaks (D4204T5) and timing belt discipline are critical. Late D4204T14 units show improved seal durability.
keyPoints: Injector seal leaks common on D4204T5 at 70k-110k miles
Timing belt must be replaced at 10 years/100k miles without exception
D4204T14 (late D4) has improved seal design
Regular oil changes with correct spec extend engine life significantly
items.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T5/D4204T14 Volvo XC60 engine prices

items.question: Where can I get a Volvo XC60 engine fitted near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes 100+ vetted UK Volvo XC60 specialists offering supply & fit services nationwide, with mobile fitters available for D4/D5 units in most regions.
keyPoints: Enter your registration to see local specialist availability
Hybrid T8 units require certified high-voltage technicians
Mobile fitting available for diesel units in many postcodes
All network suppliers are rated and reviewed by real XC60 owners
items.cta: Find Volvo XC60 engine supply & fit deals in your area

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and used XC60 engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned Volvo XC60 engines are stripped, machined and rebuilt with new wear components plus warranty; used engines are donor units sold as-is with limited coverage and lower upfront cost.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: new pistons/rings/bearings, 12-month warranty
Used: as-removed from donor, 3-6 month parts-only warranty
Reconditioned cost £1,450-£8,900; used £950-£3,200
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or critical applications
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Volvo XC60 engine options
